metadata.title: Product Experiences
metadata.description: Outcome-based product experiences for the Internal domain, focused on revenue visibility, governed execution, and internal operating leverage.

definitions.productBrick: A reusable implementation-facing building block used to realize one or more product experiences.
definitions.productCapability: A high-level, outcome-based experience that delivers customer or business value.

metadata.flowModelNote: Each experience now carries only experience-specific flows. Each flow step lists the bricks and external systems used inside that experience.

capabilities.name: See One Revenue Picture
capabilities.description: Help revenue operations users work from a single, trusted view of accounts, opportunities, and revenue data.

flows.title: See One Revenue Picture
flows.description: Help revenue operations users work from a single, trusted view of accounts, opportunities, and revenue data.

steps.title: Open revenue workspace
keyFacts: Open revenue workspace is a meaningful step in the revenue operations user experience flow.
Users need clear guidance and feedback here to keep moving forward.
painPoints: Users drop out when open revenue workspace feels fragmented across channels.
Confidence decreases when the product does not explain what happens next.

capabilities.name: Move Work Through Approvals
capabilities.description: Help internal users move work forward through required approvals and exception paths without losing context.

flows.title: Move Work Through Approvals
flows.description: Help internal users move work forward through required approvals and exception paths without losing context.

steps.title: Submit workflow step
keyFacts: This step converts intent into a real transaction or commitment.
Trust, clarity, and feedback matter more here than feature breadth.
painPoints: Users hesitate when they do not trust the outcome, price, or handoff.
Small friction at this point creates disproportionate conversion loss.

dependencies.description: Triggers required steps, checklists, and approvals by deal type and amount.

steps.title: Review finding
keyFacts: This step shapes first relevance and perceived product quality.
Users decide quickly whether the marketplace understands their intent.
painPoints: Low relevance or weak filtering creates early abandonment.
Users lose trust when the result set feels noisy or poorly explained.

dependencies.description: Runs repeatable tests and flags failures or anomalies for review.
dependencies.how: Uses Automated controls testing for runs repeatable tests and flags failures or anomalies for review in review finding.

steps.title: Launch remediation action
keyFacts: This step determines how quickly users reach initial value.
It often combines setup effort, policy choices, and correctness checks.
painPoints: Users stall when setup asks for too much context too early.
Errors here create rework and reduce confidence in the product model.
